spec.title: Self-contained DB test and scale smoke recipe

humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Scale smoke summary fields are persisted, db-wait uses Docker Compose pg_isready, ci-itotori tears down Compose, and stale no-DB reports are cleared. Dollar-sign credential escaping in generated Compose env files remains a P2 hardening follow-up.

findings.title: Compose env generation is not dollar-safe for credentials
findings.description: The Compose env writer uses double-quoted JSON string output for decoded DATABASE_URL values. Docker Compose interpolates dollar expressions inside double quotes, so credentials such as pa$word can be changed when Compose reads the env file.

evidence.detail: Re-audit verified a temporary Compose env file with a dollar sign credential was interpolated by docker compose config.

findings.impact: Public defaults are unaffected, but developers using non-default Postgres credentials with dollar signs can get a silently wrong disposable database password.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Generated Compose env files preserve dollar signs and other env-file metacharacters in decoded DATABASE_URL components.
Tests cover passwords containing dollar signs, quotes, spaces, and backslashes.
Docs describe any remaining credential-character limitations.

proposedDagNode.title: Dollar-safe Compose DB env generation
proposedDagNode.summary: Harden disposable Itotori DB Compose env generation so decoded DATABASE_URL credentials with dollar signs or env-file metacharacters survive docker compose parsing unchanged.
deliverables: Dollar-safe env-file encoder for Compose variables
Fixture tests for dollar signs, quotes, spaces, and backslashes in credentials
Docs for supported DATABASE_URL credential characters
acceptanceCriteria: docker compose config preserves generated DB credentials containing dollar signs.
Credential values with quotes, spaces, and backslashes are either preserved or rejected with semantic diagnostics.
Existing public no-secret defaults keep working.
